checks.a_analysis: The HTML conversion successfully maintains the logical flow of the original slide presentation. It uses a single-column layout which appropriately represents the sequence of the source PDF pages. Content headings and bulleted lists are preserved in their original order. The addition of a Table of Contents is an expected and acceptable navigation feature for HTML output.

The data table from page 16 of the PDF ('Improvement from Stop Consolidation') is rendered correctly in the HTML. The columns (2008-09, 2009-10*, Estimated Change) and rows are correctly aligned, and the content is clearly readable as a table.

The conversion fails significantly in rendering visual content. Numerous critical images, diagrams, and maps from the source PDF are missing or broken in the HTML output. Specifically, images on pages 3, 6, 11, and 24 are replaced by large green rectangular blocks. Diagrams (page 8), software screenshots (pages 7, 9, 10, 13, 17), and maps (pages 12, 14, 15, 22, 23) are entirely absent from the rendered view, often replaced only by text placeholders or alt-text descriptions. The line chart on page 19 is also missing, with only a small table containing its callout data present instead. This systematic loss of visual information in a slide-based document is a major defect.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF contains complex visual elements including a 'TIMS System' diagram (page 8), a 'NCDOT Railroad Crossing Report' map (page 12), and three 'Pen Plotter' photos (page 6).
checks.b_evidence_output: In the HTML, the TIMS diagram is replaced by text: '[Diagram showing components of the TIMS System...]'; the Pen Plotters section shows a large green block; maps and software screenshots throughout are missing.
checks.b_evidence_location: Throughout the document, notably under sections 'A Bit of History', 'Pen Plotters', 'The TIMS System', and 'NCDOT Railroad Crossing Report'.

checks.a_analysis: There is no evidence of unintended content duplication in the body of the document. While headings appear in both the Table of Contents and the body, this is intentional accessible design. The repetition of the title slide at the end of the document is faithful to the source PDF (page 24 is a duplicate of page 1).
